zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger de Beauchamp於大約1335年出生在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克時，他的父親Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick23歲，母親Katherine Mortimer23歲。他於1370年之前與Sybyll de Pateshulle結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1371年7月29日在法國洛易-吉黑波旁_享年37歲，埋葬在法國洛易-吉黑波旁_。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger de Beauchamp于大约1335年出生在英国瓦維其可爾沃里克时，他的父亲Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick23岁，母亲Katherine Mortimer23岁。他于1370年之前与Sybyll de Pateshulle结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1371年7月29日在法国洛易-吉黑波旁_享年37岁，埋葬在法国洛易-吉黑波旁_。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger de Beauchamp was born about 1335, in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, his father, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, was 23 and his mother, Katherine Mortimer, was 23. He married Sybyll de Pateshulle before 1370. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 29 July 1371, in Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, France, at the age of 37, and was buried in Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger de Beauchamp est né environ 1335, à Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Katherine Mortimer, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Sybyll de Pateshulle avant 1370. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 29 juillet 1371, à Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, France, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterré à Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger de Beauchamp um 1335 in Warwick, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick 23 und seine Mutter Katherine Mortimer 23. Er hat vor 1370 Sybyll de Pateshulle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 29. Juli 1371 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, Frankreich und wurde in Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de Beauchamp è nato il circa 1335 nel Warwick, Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Katherine Mortimer, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Sybyll de Pateshulle prima del 1370. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 29 luglio 1371, nel Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, Francia, all'età di 37 anni, e fu sepolto a Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger de Beauchampが約1335年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwickは23歳，母Katherine Mortimerは23歳であった。1370年以前にSybyll de Pateshulleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1371年7月29日にフランスロイル-エト-チャーベンドoメで37歳で死去し，フランスロイル-エト-チャーベンドoメにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1335년 영국 워윅실르 워윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Mortimer(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1370년 이전 Sybyll de Pateshulle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1371년 7월 29일 프랑스 로이-트-처 븐드o에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 로이-트-처 븐드o에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1335, em Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Mortimer, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Sybyll de Pateshulle antes 1370. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 29 de julho de 1371, em Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, França, com 37 anos, e foi sepultado em Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger de Beauchamp родился около 1335 г. в местности варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Katherine Mortimer, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Sybyll de Pateshulle перед 1370 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 29 июля 1371 г. в местности вендoм, лоир-ет-чир, Франция в возрасте 37 лет и был похоронен в местности вендoм, лоир-ет-чир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger de Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1335, en Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de Beauchamp 11th Earl of Warwick, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Katherine Mortimer, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Sybyll de Pateshulle antes de 1370. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 29 de julio de 1371, en Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, Francia, a la edad de 37 años, y fue sepultado en Vendôme, Loir-et-Cher, Francia.
